articles.title: ADOBE REPORTEDLY ACQUIRES TEXT-TO-VIDEO AI PLATFORM REPHRASE
articles.summary: Adobe has circulated an internal memo announcing the acquisition of California-headquartered text-to-video company Rephrase. The move is expected to bolster Adobe’s suite of Creative Cloud products. In particular, Rephrase will help the company empower its customers with the ability to generate professional-grade videos with just text.

articles.title: OFF/SCRIPT LAUNCHES AN APP TO CREATE AND BUY AI-DESIGNED FASHION
articles.summary: Off/Script is an AI platform that helps anyone conceptualize, share, and monetize product mock-ups. Creators can design anything, including clothing, high-end handbags, and decor. For the most popular designs, the Montreal-based startup funds manufactures and ships them.

articles.title: DEV MODE: BUILDING A DESIGN TOOL THAT WORKS HARDER FOR DEVELOPERS
articles.summary: Figma's Dev Mode, enhanced by acquiring Visly, offers a developer-focused tool within the design platform. It facilitates seamless collaboration between designers and developers, breaking down traditional workflow barriers and improving efficiency in the design-to-development process.

articles.title: AN EX-GOOGLE MAPS DESIGNER DOESN'T LIKE ITS REDESIGN
articles.summary: A former team member who helped design the first version of Google Maps shares her opinion on why the recent redesign doesn't work. She also describes what could be improved and how Google missed a golden opportunity to step it up.

articles.title: DESIGN SPRINTS ARE NOT ENOUGH, PREPARE FOR A UX MARATHON
articles.summary: By embracing a UX marathon mindset, designers and product specialists can reshape the narrative, driving UX from the periphery to the core of business decisions. Such companies believe that product design does not end after the development and launch of the digital service application but that the product must be constantly improved and updated based on user feedback.

articles.title: MOVE FAST, BREAK PEOPLE?
articles.summary: Creating negative urgency in design risks compromising the overall user experience, potentially losing users and damaging the company's reputation irreparably. In organizations, ultimately this could lead to staff retention issues and even compromised employee brand reputation.

articles.title: UI FACES
articles.summary: A growing library of free, AI-generated, high-resolution avatars for design mockups, thoughtfully curated to suit all your creative needs. Now also comes with a plugin for Figma.

articles.title: DRAW STUFF FASTER WITH AI
articles.summary: Auto Draw guesses what you're trying to draw and even suggests previous drawings that look like yours.

articles.title: COLOR CONTRAST CHECKER
articles.summary: Check the contrast between different color combinations against WCAG standards.

articles.title: SYSTEM USABILITY SCALE FOR DATA-DRIVEN UX
articles.summary: The SUS, a questionnaire-based method to assess the usability of a system or product, has become an indispensable component in the realm of UX, shedding light on the user-friendliness and overall quality of digital platforms. It enhances user-centered evaluation, provides quantitative measurement, offers speed and simplicity, facilitates iterative design, supports decision-making, promotes consistency, encourages user feedback, and aids in resource allocation.

articles.title: BRAILLE VS. BOSTON LINE TYPE: HOW DESIGN CAN TRULY BE INCLUSIVE
articles.summary: Louis Braille created his namesake reading system for the visually impaired in 1824. Samuel Gridley Howe developed the Boston Line Type in 1835 following a life-changing accident that blinded him. Based on the Roman alphabet, the Boston Line Type proved too complex to use effectively, leading to over a century’s delay in the adoption of the more user-friendly Braille system in the US.

articles.title: ACCESS REPORT: THE STATE OF PRODUCT ONBOARDING
articles.summary: In this interview, ex-Miro Growth leaders Kate Syuma and Viktoria Kharlamova discuss their comprehensive Onboarding Report for 2023. Their research deeply explores what makes onboarding empowered, how different strategies work for different types of businesses (B2B and B2C), and what we can learn from successful and unsuccessful onboarding efforts.

articles.title: 100 BOOKS TO FUEL DESIGN GROWTH
articles.summary: A collection of 100 books recommended by the design community categorized into nine thematic focus areas: user experience, interface and interaction, graphic design, product, theory & principles, business & strategy, creativity & innovation, tech & society, and career.

articles.title: THE BEST INVENTIONS OF 2023
articles.summary: This is a list of this year's best inventions. The selection includes 200 innovations, ranging from AI to Wellness.

articles.title: YOUR WEBSITE’S URLS CAN AND SHOULD BE BEAUTIFUL
articles.summary: A well-crafted URL gives users a sense of what they will find before visiting it and provides human-readable context. A pretty URL makes your site more accessible, user-friendly, and SEO-effective.
